results.title: 1777 Hotline Emergency Screening Protocols and Cost Implications for Non-Emergency Ambulance Service Case Redirection
results.summary: The 1777 hotline employs a structured screening protocol to identify life-threatening conditions such as major trauma, stroke, or cardiac arrest, directing these cases to the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) unless the caller declines. Between 2024 and 2025, an average of 1,420 cases were annually redirected from 1777 to SCDF. Callers are informed upfront that 1777 is for non-emergency conveyance; however, if a caller insists on a private ambulance despite emergency advice, a licensed Emergency Ambulance Service provider will be dispatched. For non-emergency cases, a Medical Transport Service is sent, which may activate SCDF upon arrival if the condition worsens. Fees for private ambulance services are subject to operator terms and may apply even if the case is later redirected to SCDF.

results.title: Accident Statistics for Past Three Years and Traffic Safety Measures against Speeding and Yellow Box Violations at Loyang Avenue and Old Tampines Road Junction
results.summary: Between 2023 and 2025, 13 accidents occurred at the Loyang Avenue and Old Tampines Road junction. To improve safety, the Land Transport Authority will enhance traffic light visibility and install additional speed limit signs within the next few months. The Traffic Police have deployed speed alert signs along Loyang Avenue. While enforcement cameras are typically used at accident-prone locations, space constraints from ongoing construction make installation impractical at this junction. Consequently, the Traffic Police will increase manned enforcement as an interim measure instead of installing cameras.

results.title: Additional Measures to Support Sustainability of Individual Stallholder System
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding additional measures for the Individual Stallholder System and 2030 adoption targets for the Central Kitchen Meal Model was addressed in the Ministry's oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 14 to 20 on 3 February 2026. No new measures, timelines, or specific expectations were announced during this session. The response directs users to the official parliamentary record for the detailed answers provided on that date.

results.title: AI Adoption's Impact on Employment of Fresh Graduates
results.summary: The Minister stated that while AI's specific impact on entry-level PMET jobs remains uncertain, the Government is monitoring trends and collaborating with employers and NTUC to address emerging challenges. To enhance graduate employability amidst 2025 economic uncertainties, the GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT) Programme was introduced to provide industry-relevant experience and facilitate transitions to permanent employment. Existing initiatives include the AI Apprenticeship Programme under AI Strategy 2.0, WSG's Jobs Transformation Maps for guiding role redesign, and reskilling support via IMDA's Tech Skills Accelerator and WSG's Career Conversion Programmes. These measures aim to integrate AI competencies into IHL curricula and support human-centric job redesign where AI augments rather than displaces human input.

results.title: Addressing Concerns Relating to Public Charger Quality and Charger Interoperability Issues to Encourage Continued Purchase of Electric Vehicles
results.summary: Demand for electric cars remains strong, with 45% of 2025 registrations being electric. The Ministry addresses quality and interoperability concerns by reviewing regulations to ensure national charging standard relevance and enforcing licensing requirements for public charger operators to maintain a minimum 90% service uptime. Additionally, the Ministry collaborates with the industry to trial new technologies through regulatory sandboxes. These measures support growing electric vehicle adoption while keeping pace with the latest charging technology to ensure Singapore remains on track for The Green Plan.

results.title: Adjournment of Debate
results.summary: Mr Jeffrey Siow moved to adjourn the debate on the specified item. The Speaker noted the motion would be considered the following day. No substantive policy measures, legislative changes, or operational constraints were discussed during this procedural segment. The transcript contains no new statistics, eligibility rules, or timeline updates beyond the scheduling of the next debate session.

results.title: Allowing E-polling via SingPass for Home Improvement Programme and Extending Programme to HDB Blocks Built After 1997
results.summary: The Housing and Development Board (HDB) will extend the Home Improvement Programme (HIP) to flats built after 1997 once the selection of older pre-1997 blocks is substantially completed, with the exact timeline contingent on budget availability and industry capacity. To improve voting convenience, residents unable to attend polling may appoint a proxy; however, the Ministry will not immediately adopt electronic polling via SingPass, as further study is required to ensure voting integrity, system reliability, and cybersecurity. HDB announces selected blocks approximately one and a half years before commencement to allow residents to plan renovations, though actual durations vary based on tender processes, resident engagement, and site-specific complexities.

results.title: Allowing HDB Homeowners to Replace Entrance Doors with Fire-rated Ones under Home Improvement Programme
results.summary: HDB blocks are designed per the Fire Code, providing fire-rated entrance doors only where required to minimize fire spread. Under the Home Improvement Programme, flats receive fire-rated doors solely when mandated by the Code, ensuring regulatory compliance and financial prudence. Doors are not fire-rated if located more than three metres from an exit staircase and facing an external corridor, as such corridors facilitate heat and smoke dispersion. This approach balances safety requirements with cost efficiency for homeowners.

results.title: Alternative Solutions or Public Space Designation Plans for Youth Arts Activities at Bayfront MRT Station
results.summary: The Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) provides free dance spaces at six ActiveSG Sport Centres and open locations with full-length mirrors in The Kallang precinct, while the National Arts Council (NAC) activates MRT stations like Bayfront via the Super 24 Training Grounds initiative for rehearsals. MCCY is redeveloping Somerset Belt as a youth precinct under the SG Youth Plan and utilizes community centres through ArtsEverywhere@CDC for performance opportunities. The underground linkway at Bayfront MRT is privately owned by Marina Bay Sands. When assessing public space usage, the Ministry balances accessibility, safety, impact on other users, and intended purpose to support youth arts activities without adopting deterrent measures.

results.title: Annual Number of Notices of Traffic Offence Issued to Malaysia-Registered Motorcycles for 2025
results.summary: In 2025, approximately 22,000 Notices of Traffic Offence were issued to Malaysia-registered motorcycles. This figure represents the annual count for that specific vehicle category and registration jurisdiction. The Coordinating Minister for National Security and Minister for Home Affairs provided this statistic during the parliamentary session. No further breakdown by offence type or enforcement agency was stated in the response.

results.title: Appeals for Additional CDC Vouchers from Different Households Sharing Same Address
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth confirmed that unrelated Singaporean households sharing a single address remain eligible for separate Community Development Council (CDC) Vouchers per tranche. Applicants may claim vouchers at any Community Centre or Club by submitting supporting documents such as tenancy agreements or marriage certificates to verify distinct households. Applications are generally approved provided the applicants are not from the same household and have not previously claimed vouchers. The Ministry accepts various forms of proof beyond standard address-related documents to facilitate these appeals, with assistance available at nearest Community Centres for those facing difficulties.

results.title: Assessing Noise Generated by Roads, MRT Lines and Airport as Part of Transport System Design
results.summary: The Ministry assesses cumulative noise impacts from roads, MRT, and airports using simulation models and ground measurements to ensure projected levels adhere to guidelines. Mitigation strategies include maintaining buffer distances for residential areas, installing noise barriers, and routing aircraft away from homes when wind conditions allow. For Seletar Airport, a nightly curfew from 10:00 pm to 7:00 am applies, excluding medical and emergency flights, while stringent International Civil Aviation Organization requirements are enforced. These measures balance efficient transport connectivity with maintaining liveable communities.

results.title: Assessment of 11% Decline in Reading on Trust that Next Generation of Singaporeans Would be Better Off
results.summary: The Government notes the 2026 Edelman Trust Barometer shows a 11% decline in intergenerational optimism in Singapore, attributing it to global economic conditions and job concerns. Internal surveys do not indicate a comparable deterioration in young Singaporeans' outlook, while historical data confirms Singapore sustains strong social mobility compared to other advanced economies. Updated internal findings will be released in due course.

results.title: Assessment of Current Capacity and Coverage of Strength Training Facilities for Seniors
results.summary: The Ministry is expanding strength training capacity for seniors through fitness corners in HDB estates and public parks, alongside ActiveSG gyms islandwide. Seniors can access muscle-strengthening programmes organised by the Health Promotion Board and Sport Singapore, often at expanded Active Ageing Centres. These public initiatives complement private options like Gym Tonic. The Ministry will continue monitoring and supporting uptake to meet growing demand. Specific timelines for expansion or new facility counts were not stated in the response.

results.title: Assessment on Lower Floor Residents' Exposure to Secondhand Cigarette Smoke from Common Areas and Implementing Buffer Zones to Reduce Exposure
results.summary: The Ministry for Sustainability and the Environment states that smoking is prohibited in most HDB common areas, including void decks and playgrounds, to limit secondhand smoke exposure. Consequently, the Ministry has not conducted studies on resident exposure levels from these specific zones. The Government will continue to review smoking prohibition policies regularly and will consider public feedback when updating regulations. No additional buffer zones or new regulatory measures were confirmed at this time.

results.title: Assisting Vulnerable Residents to Apply for Certificate of Medical Need to Ride Mobility Scooter on Public Paths
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport directed members to the Second Reading of the Land Transport and Related Matters Bill for details on the Certificate of Medical Need requirement, set for implementation from mid-2026. The Minister did not provide specific measures or timelines for assisting vulnerable residents lacking technological means to obtain permits. No further clarification on support mechanisms or eligibility constraints was stated in the transcript.

results.title: Average Timeline of SPED School Placement Process for 2,880 Unenrolled Children
results.summary: Of the approximately 2,880 Singaporean children who did not participate in the 2025 Primary One Registration Exercise, 885 applied for Special Education (SPED) schools, with 864 already offered places. The Minister stated the average time from application to placement offer is six months, though waits may extend due to vacancy constraints or the need for further professional advice. Children with autism spectrum disorder and intellectual disability may face longer wait times as demand for these profiles has grown significantly. The Ministry is expanding capacity across the existing 26 SPED schools and working with social service agencies to attract and retain educators.

results.title: Balancing Efforts to Attract High-spending Visitors and A High Volume of Visitors
results.summary: The Singapore Tourism Board (STB) prioritises attracting high-yield visitors while sustaining visitor volume to support employment. This strategy targets high-growth segments like Meetings, Incentives, Conferences and Exhibitions (MICE) and Active Silvers, alongside families. STB partners with the industry to launch unique developments, citing Rainforest Wild ASIA and Disney Cruise Line's Disney Adventure as recent examples. The board will also strengthen its event pipeline, including MICE events such as Food & Hospitality Asia, leisure events like the Formula 1 Singapore Grand Prix, and upcoming A-list acts like BTS.

results.title: Breakdown of Government-funded PhD Scholars in Science Disciplines by Citizenship over Past Five Years and Bond Completion Rates
results.summary: Over the past decade, approximately 6,000 STEM PhD students enrolled annually in autonomous universities, with Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents comprising about one-third of the population. About 90% of these students receive government funding, with stipends set at $4,300 for Citizens, $3,900 for Permanent Residents, and $3,500 for international students. Students fulfill service obligations through teaching and research supervision during their candidature. The Minister did not provide specific five-year award counts, average values by group, completion failure rates, or default recovery mechanisms.

results.title: Breakdown of PSLE Results by Percentage of Achievement Level Scoring Bands for Past Three Years
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that the percentage of students achieving Achievement Level 6 or better in PSLE standard subjects has remained consistently high from 2023 to 2025. Specifically, at least nine in ten students attained this standard in English, Mother Tongue, Mathematics, and Science during this period. The response did not provide the specific percentage breakdowns requested for each subject individually, nor did it include data for the year 2026.

results.title: Breakdown of PhD Students in Science Disciplines at Autonomous Universities by Citizenship for Past Five Years
results.summary: Over the past decade, an average of 6,000 PhD students annually enroll in STEM courses at autonomous universities, with Singapore Citizens and Permanent Residents comprising a stable one-third of the population. Approximately 90% of these students are Government-Funded via tuition subsidies and stipends, where Citizens receive $4,300 monthly, compared to $3,900 for Permanent Residents and $3,500 for international students. All PhD students fulfill teaching and research supervision duties as a service obligation during their candidature. The Minister did not provide specific data for the past five years or a comparison to figures from 20 years ago.

results.title: Breakdown of SkillsFuture Credit Utilisation by Age
results.summary: As of end-2025, the overall SkillsFuture Credit utilisation rate stood at approximately 46%. Specific breakdowns by age group (25–40, 40–55, and 55+) were referenced in a table provided by the Minister for Education but are not included in this transcript. The response confirms the aggregate figure while indicating that detailed age-specific data exists in the accompanying visual aid. No further elaboration on the specific percentages for each cohort was provided in the text.

results.title: CREST and COMIT Referral Statistics by Source in Past Three Years and Assessment on Appropriate Care Tiers
results.summary: The Ministry of Health does not track referral registration status but established minimum qualifications for Community Outreach Teams (CREST) and Community Intervention Team (COMIT) staff effective 2025 to ensure competency. Between 2022 and 2024, CREST supported an average of 19,000 clients annually, with referrals comprising 46% self-referrals, 41% from other agencies, and 13% step-down from hospitals; COMIT supported 14,000 clients annually with 41% step-down, 36% from other agencies, and 23% self-referrals. Clients receive six to seven sessions for CREST and five to 11 for COMIT, with average programme durations of 1.5 years and one year respectively. Referral appropriateness is assessed against the Practice Guide for Tiered Care Model for Mental Health, while the Agency for Integrated Care monitors service capacity to sustainably resourcemanpower and meet resident needs.

results.title: Circumstances for Suspension or Termination of Caterer's Operations under Central Kitchen Meal Model
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the specific circumstances for suspending or terminating a caterer's operations under the Central Kitchen Meal Model, along with contingency protocols to ensure student meal access, were previously addressed in the Ministry's oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 14 to 20 on 3 February 2026. No new details regarding these measures or protocols were provided during this session. The response refers users to the existing parliamentary record for the full information on operational constraints and continuity plans.

results.title: Clarification by Senior Minister of State for Sustainability and the Environment
results.summary: Senior Minister of State for Sustainability and the Environment Dr Janil Puthueary clarified a pricing error regarding beverage container producers under the scheme. The Minister corrected a previous statement of "3% to 4%" to the accurate figure of "three to four cents per container." This adjustment applies to producers of 80% of registered beverage containers, who will face the specified price increase. No other policy changes or timelines were mentioned in this clarification.

results.title: Closing Gaps to Support Caregivers' Long-term Financial Security and Re-employment Pathways
results.summary: Caregivers returning to work can access Workforce Singapore and NTUC's Employment and Employability Institute programmes, including Career Conversion and Mid-Career Pathways, alongside SkillsFuture training subsidies. The Majulah Package's December 2024 Retirement Savings Bonus and January 2025 enhancements to the Silver Support and Matched Retirement Savings Schemes aim to boost retirement adequacy for seniors with caregiving duties. Eligible working caregivers may also receive the Workfare Income Supplement and Earn and Save Bonus to supplement income and savings. The Minister did not specify future plans to close remaining gaps in long-term financial security or employment re-entry pathways beyond these existing measures.

results.title: Community Health Post-Expansion and Heartland Public Health Screening Programme Plans
results.summary: The Minister clarified that Community Health Posts complement family doctor care rather than duplicating it. Under the Healthier SG initiative, eligible Singapore Citizens can access fully subsidised screenings at their enrolled primary care provider. The Ministry encourages residents to utilise family doctors for health assessments, screening recommendations, result explanations, and follow-up guidance. Specific plans for additional heartland public health screenings were not detailed in the response.

results.title: Complaints Received on Inaccurate Bus Arrival Information on LTA's Online and Physical Platforms
results.summary: Over the past five years, LTA received fewer than 100 monthly complaints regarding inaccurate bus arrival information. The recent ETA system disruption stemmed from memory cache build-up in on-board equipment, causing communication errors that prevented correct location display on apps and physical platforms. Remediation involved manually re-uploading firmware and replacing transmitters to fully restore the system. LTA is currently examining ways to enhance reliability, including evaluating the feasibility of over-the-air transmissions for future software updates to reduce commuter inconvenience and prevent recurrence of such technical disruptions.

results.title: Complaints on Pest Bird Nuisance and Measures to Reduce Such Nuisances in Last Five Years
results.summary: Over the last five years, an annual average of about 22,000 feedback cases on pest birds was received. NParks, in collaboration with the National Environment Agency, Singapore Food Agency, Town Councils, and Singapore Police Force, implemented a multi-pronged strategy. Measures include reducing human-generated food sources, enhancing trapping and removal methods, enforcing against illegal feeding, and resuming crow shooting. The agencies also conduct education and outreach efforts to mitigate disamenities and safeguard public safety. The Ministry continues to adopt these measures to effectively manage pest bird nuisance.

results.title: Consideration of Alternative Sites before Forest was Cleared for Jurong Innovation District's CleanTech Park and Bahar Precincts
results.summary: The Government assessed various sites for the Jurong Innovation District's CleanTech Park and Bahar precincts, selecting locations integrated with existing Bulim and upcoming Tengah Industrial Estate sites to support advanced manufacturing and industry-academia collaboration. An Environmental Impact Assessment identified mitigation measures including retaining 14.5 hectares of high ecological value forest, transplanting conservation-significant plant species, and implementing wildlife management plans, which JTC will adopt as practicable. The development aims to create approximately 95,000 jobs and incorporate green features like the five-hectare Potter's Garden sanctuary to integrate green spaces and celebrate local heritage within the industrial developments.

results.title: Considerations for Pro-rating ABSD Remission Timeline for Developers by Size of Residential Development
results.summary: The Minister confirmed that ABSD remission timelines for licensed developers were extended in March 2025 to ensure timely housing supply release. This relief applies specifically to complex projects, defined as large en bloc redevelopments yielding at least 700 residential units or where new unit counts are 1.5 times the existing development. The Government has not considered pro-rating these timelines by Gross Floor Area or projected unit numbers. The current eligibility criteria remain fixed for qualifying large-scale developments.

results.title: Considerations in Evaluating Invitations for Singapore to Join New Multilateral Groups Such as Board of Peace
results.summary: The Singapore Government is currently assessing an invitation from the United States to join the Board of Peace. Minister for Foreign Affairs Vivian Balakrishnan confirmed this evaluation is underway but did not specify the criteria for consideration or confirm whether a wait-and-see approach will be adopted. Details regarding the specific considerations for joining new multilateral groups were not provided in the response.

results.title: Construction Status and Expected Completion for Tampines North Integrated Transport Hub
results.summary: The Tampines North Integrated Transport Hub is scheduled for completion in 2030, with construction commencing in 2024. The timeline was extended by one year due to a delay in the Cross Island Line station caused by the COVID-19 pandemic. As an integrated development linking bus interchanges with MRT stations and commercial areas, it requires more time than single-use projects. Residents currently utilize the existing Tampines North Bus Interchange while the hub is under construction.

results.title: Cooperation with Foreign Jurisdictions to Disrupt Cross-border Vape Supply Chains
results.summary: The Health Sciences Authority (HSA), Immigration and Checkpoints Authority (ICA), and Singapore Police Force (SPF) collaborate with foreign counterparts to disrupt cross-border vape supply chains. In 2025, joint operations detected 59 smuggling cases, seizing approximately 230,000 e-vaporisers, while over 10,000 online advertisements were removed in 2024 and 2025. About 99% of takedowns involved overseas platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, WeChat, YouTube, and Telegram, with the remainder hosted on local sites like Carousel and Lazada. Authorities employ daily bot surveillance, officer reviews, and public tip-offs to identify offending sites, subsequently blocking them in coordination with the Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA) and the Online Criminal Harms Act Office.

results.title: Coordinating Befrienders' Outreach to the Elderly and Scaling Up Use of Technology to Monitor Seniors' Well-being
results.summary: Under Age Well SG, the Silver Generation Office coordinates with social service agencies to ensure befriending services maintain monthly contact with accepted seniors. The Housing and Development Board progressively extends the wireless Alert Alarm System to public rental block residents for emergency assistance. Additionally, the Ministry of Health will implement an enhanced Home Personal Care service islandwide from April 2026, allowing eligible seniors to subscribe to technology-enabled fall and incident monitoring and response services to support vulnerable individuals living alone.

results.title: Cost-benefit Analysis for Escrow Deposits in Private Hire Car and Taxi Rental Industry
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that imposing escrow deposits on private hire car and taxi rental companies would increase operational costs due to the need for additional working capital. Consequently, these costs would likely be passed on to hirers through higher rental rates and to passengers via increased fares. The Government has not considered this measure, as the financial burden on operators and the resulting price increases for consumers outweigh the potential benefits.

results.title: Criteria for Implementing Free Parking Scheme in New HDB Estates Such as Tengah
results.summary: The Free Parking Scheme (FPS) at HDB car parks offers free parking from 7.00am to 10.30pm on Sundays and public holidays to promote social interaction. Implementation occurs only after resident parking demand stabilises and sufficient lots exist for visitors. HDB is monitoring Tengah's new car parks and will progressively introduce FPS starting 1 February 2026. The Minister confirmed this timeline based on current demand monitoring, noting that other new estates follow similar criteria once conditions are met.

results.title: Current Healthcare Workforce Strength Compared against Projected Service Demand
results.summary: The Ministry projects the healthcare workforce will grow by 20%, from 129,000 in 2024 to approximately 156,000 in 2030, to meet evolving care models and demand. This expansion includes growth in community nurses and other areas. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies and Minister for Health stated that the current workforce is broadly adequate for population demand, with low overall vacancy rates in public hospitals. Medium to long-term manpower plans are regularly reviewed and translated into education, training, and recruitment initiatives to address future needs.

results.title: Daily Entry Statistics of Malaysia-registered Motorcycles and Data on their Involvement in Road Safety Incidents in 2025
results.summary: In 2025, approximately 69,000 Malaysia-registered motorcycles entered Singapore daily on average, with figures varying monthly. Of these, about 1,600 were involved in road safety incidents in Singapore during the year. The Acting Minister for Transport provided these statistics without specifying accident severity or breakdown by month. No further details on specific incidents or mitigation measures were stated in the response.

results.title: Data Related to Marriage Status, Parental Age and Number of Children in Single-parent Households
results.summary: Based on data from 2020 to 2024, there is an average of 83,000 single-parent resident households in Singapore. Of these, 58% are divorced or separated, 39% are widowed, and 3% are never-married parents. Regarding household composition, 54% have one child, 35% have two children, and 11% have three or more. The Minister directed Members to the SingStat website for detailed yearly statistics on single-parent household demographics, including parental age and the age band of the youngest child.

results.title: Data on Children under Non-Parental Care Due to Parent Incapacity
results.summary: As of 2024, 1,454 children in out-of-home care were placed due to parental incapacity or protection concerns, with 386 residing in kinship care arrangements such as those with grandparents or relatives. These placements are temporary, covering scenarios including parental incarceration, until safety assessments permit family reunification. The Minister clarified that care types include both family-based and residential options. Specific data on parental unwillingness versus incapacity was not separately quantified in the response.

results.title: Data on Foreign Motorists Given Fines in 2024 and 2025
results.summary: Foreign-registered vehicles incurred 86,492 fines in 2024 and 126,820 in 2025 for offences including driving, parking, and emissions. In 2024, fine distribution was 26% for cars, 41% for motorcycles, and 33% for other vehicles; in 2025, it shifted to 20% for cars, 38% for motorcycles, and 42% for other vehicles. Multi-agency operations at land checkpoints in October 2025 targeted motorists with outstanding fines, requiring settlement before entry. The Immigration and Checkpoints Authority may deny entry to foreign vehicles with unpaid fines. The Minister confirmed agencies regularly conduct these operations but did not state whether further enforcement efforts are planned beyond the stated checkpoint procedures.

results.title: Data on Leave Days taken by Parents Due to Childcare Centre Closures or Children Falling Ill
results.summary: The Government does not track specific reasons for taking Government-Paid Childcare Leave (GPCL) and lacks data on leave usage exceeding entitlements due to childcare centre closures or children falling ill. In 2023, 58% of eligible working mothers and 53% of fathers took more than three days of GPCL, with the vast majority of this group utilizing all six available days. Employers only claim reimbursement for parents taking more than three days, limiting available data to this subset. Data for 2024 is not yet available. Beyond leave provisions, the Government introduced Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests to help parents manage caregiving responsibilities. The Government will continue reviewing parental leave provisions and collaborating with Tripartite Partners to foster family-friendly workplaces.

results.title: Data on Non-residents Residing in Singapore for More than Five Years and those Who Have Unsuccessfully Applied for Resident Status
results.summary: As of December 2025, approximately 390,000 non-residents have resided in Singapore for over five consecutive years, primarily as work permit holders and migrant domestic workers. The Ministry does not track unsuccessful Permanent Residence applications, noting that most of this group, particularly work permit holders, are restricted from setting roots and unlikely to qualify for PR. For those wishing to integrate, the Ministry suggests engaging with community values through heritage visits, participating in grassroots programmes like cultural festivals and sports, and volunteering where pass conditions allow. The Government maintains that limited resources must be focused strategically and regularly engages both locals and non-residents to understand sentiments regarding this issue.

results.title: Data on Profile of Persons with Forfeited SkillsFuture Credits and Total Value Involved
results.summary: The one-off SkillsFuture Credit top-up of $500 provided to eligible Singaporeans aged 25 and above in 2020 expired by the end of 2025 after a five-year validity period. Approximately 40% of eligible recipients utilized the credit before expiry, with usage rising to around 45% among those aged 30 to 60. The Ministry does not track forfeiture data by income group, noting that non-usage may stem from employer-sponsored training or a lack of perceived need for additional training. While the Government aims to encourage meaningful lifelong learning regardless of credit usage, no nationwide survey was announced to study factors influencing credit utilisation.

results.title: Data on Singaporeans Categorised as NEETs by Demographics
results.summary: The NEET indicator tracks early-life labour market vulnerabilities for youths aged 15 to 24, excluding older age groups where factors like caregiving and health shape participation. Singaporean NEET numbers declined from 17,100 in 2023 to 15,900 in 2025, with an average duration of 6 months indicating generally transitory spells. Data on the percentage of Singaporean NEETs aged 40 and above remaining outside the workforce for more than 12 months is not tracked. Instead, sustained detachment for this older demographic is monitored using long-term unemployment, labour force participation, and economic inactivity indicators, which better capture their specific labour market circumstances.

results.title: Data on Student Care Centres Not Registered as Fee Assistance Administrator or School-based Centre and Regulation over Such Centres
results.summary: All school-based Student Care Centres are registered with the Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF). Non-school-based centres must register only if they wish to administer the Student Care Fee Assistance scheme. Regardless of registration status, all centres and staff must comply with prevailing laws, including the Children and Young Persons Act. MSF will continue monitoring the regulatory framework and will enhance it if necessary. The Minister did not confirm plans to subject all centres to a licensing regime similar to early childhood development centres.

results.title: Deployment of Community Relations Unit Powers beyond Pilot Run in Tampines for Severe Hoarding Cases
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the Hoarding Management Core Group (HMCG) coordinates multi-agency responses to severe hoarding cases based on public health and safety severity. Interventions include National Environment Agency directives for infestation control and Singapore Civil Defence Force actions to clear exit obstructions and ban flammable items. The Minister did not confirm accelerating Community Relations Unit powers beyond the Tampines pilot or specific Budget 2026 resourcing for this expansion. The New Environment Action Team continues early identification and support efforts.

results.title: Details on Stepped-up Surveillance for Central Kitchens, Including Different Classification for those Supplying School Meals
results.summary: Since August 2025, the Singapore Food Agency (SFA) has stepped up surveillance for central kitchens supplying school meals under the Ministry of Education's Central Kitchen Meal Model programme. These facilities are already subject to frequent inspections due to their large scale and graded under the Safety Assurance for Food Establishment (SAFE) framework, with government procurement now considering SAFE grades from Phase 1 (19 January 2026) to ensure a proper track record. SFA requires licensed ingredient sourcing, segregation of raw and ready-to-eat foods, identification of critical control points, and appointment of a food hygiene officer to prevent cross-contamination. Beyond inspections, SFA collaborates with the Communicable Diseases Agency and schools to strengthen food safety management across preparation, transportation, and distribution, noting that outbreaks often involve multiple factors beyond the central kitchen itself.

results.title: Detecting Bullying Incidents and Strengthening Reporting Channels in view of Smartphone Usage Restrictions in Schools
results.summary: Schools employ self-reporting, peer reporting, and teacher observations to detect bullying, utilizing evidence like visible marks and eyewitness testimony which remain unaffected by smartphone usage restrictions. Online platforms and regular check-in conversations serve as reporting channels, while student-provided videos are not treated as sole evidence due to their one-sided nature. The Ministry of Education does not track the number of bullying cases substantiated via student-provided videos and has not committed to publishing comparative bullying data before and after the smartphone usage restrictions.

results.title: Digital Break and Cyber Wellness Programmes for Students in Light of Social Media and Gaming Addiction Trend
results.summary: The Ministry of Education introduced Cyber Wellness lessons in Character and Citizenship Education to teach students how to balance online and offline activities, manage social media usage, and recognize digital risks. Starting in 2026, all primary and secondary schools will prohibit the use of smartphones and smartwatches during school hours, including Co-Curricular Activities. While existing programmes like the annual "Safer Internet Day" encourage screen-free time, the Minister noted that students still require guidance to resist extended device use. Consequently, the Ministry will continue collaborating with parents and the community to share resources and strategies for guiding children in technology use, acknowledging that effective management requires a whole-of-society effort.

results.title: Due Diligence Conducted to Ensure Independence of Firms Appointed to Assess Carbon Credit Integrity
results.summary: The National Environment Agency (NEA) appointed BeZero Carbon, Calyx Global, and Sylvera via competitive tender to independently assess carbon credit methodologies under the International Carbon Credits (ICC) Framework. Selection prioritized provider independence, transparent ratings, market expertise, and cost competitiveness. NEA enforces strict oversight through mandatory disclosure of commercial relationships and regular monitoring to mitigate conflicts of interest. To prevent undue influence, work allocation is managed to exclude providers with existing financial interests from assessing specific projects. Final approvals for carbon credits remain with the Government, evaluated against Singapore's Eligibility Criteria to ensure high environmental integrity. These providers supplement, rather than replace, NEA's primary environmental integrity assessments.

results.title: Due Diligence Measures before Processing Adoption of Children
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development will review existing adoption processes to enhance due diligence measures once facts regarding recent alleged child trafficking cases in Indonesia are clearer. This review will adopt a calibrated and proportionate approach to avoid lengthening processing times or rendering overseas adoption infeasible for the majority of legitimate cases. No specific new measures or timelines have been announced at this stage, as the government is currently assessing the situation before deciding on any enhancements.

results.title: EatWise SG Performance Indicators for Detecting Malnutrition Risk among Seniors, Current Performance Levels and Annual Targets for Nutrition Screenings
results.summary: The EatWise SG initiative equips healthcare workers with training and resources to deliver nutrition advice and conduct assessments during opportunistic frailty screenings to identify seniors at malnutrition risk. As pilots remain in early stages, the Ministry of Health lacks sufficient data to report current performance levels, outcomes, or referrals for malnutrition cases. Consequently, no specific annual targets through 2030 have been established for early detection and referral metrics. Effectiveness will instead be monitored via indicators such as patient satisfaction with nutrition care and adherence to recommended diet strategies.

results.title: Effectiveness of Noise Barriers, Low-noise Pavement and Rail Modifications on Residential Noise Level Reductions
results.summary: Noise mitigation measures, including rail and road noise barriers and low-noise pavements, have reduced train and traffic noise by up to five to 10 decibels. Effectiveness is verified through post-installation measurements ensuring compliance with National Environment Agency guidelines. The Land Transport Authority will continue exploring and adopting new noise reduction technologies where feasible and effective. The response confirms the efficacy of current measures but does not specify a timeline for adopting newer technologies or standards.

results.title: Effectiveness of Voucher Schemes in Helping Singaporeans Cope with Cost-of-living Concerns Sustainably
results.summary: The Government regularly reviews voucher schemes like the GSTV and CDC vouchers to ensure sustainable cost-of-living support. Total Assurance Package support increased from $6.6 billion in 2022 to over $10 billion in 2025, fully offsetting inflation-driven expenditure rises for lower-income households and substantially offsetting them for middle-income households from 2022 to 2025. Structural measures include Workfare, the Fresh Start Housing Scheme, and Silver Support, complemented by one-off assistance where needed. Long-term sustainability relies on broad-based wage growth outpacing inflation, pursued through economic growth, job creation, and skills upgrading. The Minister stated this approach balances responsive, targeted support with fiscal prudence.

results.title: Electric Vehicle Charger Breakdown by AC versus DC Type and Kilowatt Rating
results.summary: Singapore currently operates approximately 28,300 electric vehicle charging points. Of these, 24,900 are alternating current (AC) units, with roughly 50% rated at 7.4 kW or below. The remaining 3,200 are direct current (DC) fast chargers, over 90% of which have power ratings of 50 kW or higher. Additionally, 200 points function as battery swapping stations for electric motorcycles. The Acting Minister for Transport provided these figures during the parliamentary debate on charger specifications.

results.title: Enforcing against E-vaporisers Disguised as Smart Devices or Everyday Items
results.summary: The Health Sciences Authority (HSA) has detected two cases of e-vaporisers disguised as smart devices, such as gaming consoles or USB drives, and shares intelligence with enforcement agencies to aid frontline officers in identification. While current powers are deemed adequate, the Ministry of Health plans to legislate stiffer penalties for importers and suppliers regardless of the device's form. Additionally, the use of deceptive designs to evade detection will be presented to the Courts as an aggravating factor warranting higher sentences. The Ministry intends to review legislation to introduce heavier penalties for manufacturers and distributors who employ such tactics to circumvent supervision and attract young users.

results.title: Enhancing Food Safety Measures Following Recent Gastroenteritis Cases at Primary Schools and Incentives for Operators of School Canteens
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) confirms that 95% of schools retain traditional individual stallholders, offering low rentals of $5–$15 monthly and holiday waivers, while the Central Kitchen Meal Model is piloted only in 13 schools facing severe vacancy shortages with no 2030 scaling target. Recent gastroenteritis incidents at River Valley and North View Primary Schools are under joint investigation by the Singapore Food Agency (SFA) and Communicable Diseases Agency (CDA); affected students have returned to school after enhanced cleaning. MOE states central kitchens are licensed by SFA, undergo frequent inspections, and are capped at serving four to five schools to contain risk. If investigations confirm food safety breaches, operators face suspension or termination, and MOE remains open to hybrid models or paying stallholders to sustain affordable, nutritious meals.

results.title: Enhancing Statutory Penalties to Minimise Impact of Sudden Closures of Early Childhood Development Centres
results.summary: Over the past five years, an average of 26 early childhood development centres annually failed to provide the mandatory six months' notice before ceasing operations. Under the ECDC Act, licensees must give this notice to parents and ECDA, with breaches attracting regulatory actions including warning letters, financial penalties, or future licence bars. ECDA prioritises children's continuity of care during abrupt closures, actively assisting affected parents in placing their children in alternative preschools. The Ministry will continue to reiterate compliance requirements to all operators while taking steps to mitigate impacts on families.

results.title: Ensuring Appropriate Use of NRIC Numbers across Public and Private Sector Transactions
results.summary: In January 2025, the Government announced plans to improve NRIC usage by stopping their misuse as authentication passwords and phasing out partial NRIC numbers for identification. Government agencies halted password practices over a year ago, while the Personal Data Protection Commission and Cyber Security Agency issued guidance to private sectors including telecommunications, finance, and healthcare; flagrant misuse remains penalisable under the Personal Data Protection Act. Starting with the public sector, agencies now use full NRIC numbers for accurate identification or omit them entirely where unnecessary, with private sector guidelines subject to public consultation. Since June 2025, a national campaign across multiple platforms educates the public on proper usage, encouraging continued reporting of mishandled cases during the transition period to balance data protection with legitimate business needs.

results.title: Ensuring Correct Use of Dormitory Transition Scheme Grants
results.summary: The Dormitory Transition Scheme (DTS) grant supports dormitories in retrofitting to meet interim standards by 2030, covering only three specific work categories like ensuite toilets and room partitions at set piece rates. Funding excludes existing compliant facilities or works exceeding requirements, with grant amounts calculated based on the extent of necessary retrofitting. To ensure responsible use, the Ministry of Manpower (MOM) evaluates works for qualification, disburses funds only after completion and certification by Qualified Persons, and conducts pre-disbursement inspections to verify compliance with interim standards. These safeguards prevent misuse by ensuring grants are strictly applied to eligible retrofitting projects that align with the scheme's objectives.

results.title: Ensuring Residential Liveability Near Expressways and MRT Corridors in Long-term Transport Expansion Plans
results.summary: Future transport expansion plans incorporate noise mitigation measures, such as noise barriers and sufficient buffer distances, to ensure noise levels comply with National Environment Agency guidelines. The Land Transport Authority reviews resident feedback via public channels and ground engagements to assess mitigation adequacy, taking noise measurements to determine actual levels and implementing further measures where necessary. These upfront design considerations and ongoing assessments aim to maintain residential liveability near expressways and MRT corridors.

results.title: Error Rate in Sex-sorting Process for Mosquitoes in Project Wolbachia and Process to Ensure These Mosquitoes Lack Physiological Capability to Bite Humans
results.summary: No biting reports have been received from male Wolbachia mosquitoes since 2024, while the sex-sorting process maintains a 99.9% accuracy rate. The National Environment Agency confirms that released males lack the physiological capability to bite humans, supported by independent risk assessments and international research. Although local female mosquitoes may still bite residents, released females show significantly reduced disease transmission ability. Project Wolbachia sites have achieved an 80% reduction in female Aedes aegypti populations and over 70% reduction in dengue risk. Source reduction efforts remain important to suppress the overall mosquito population alongside these biological controls.

results.title: Estimated Carbon Footprint and Air Pollutant Emissions from Firework Displays at Major Events
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that fireworks events are transient and infrequent, meaning they are not a main source of carbon emissions or air pollutants in Singapore. Consequently, the Government does not track carbon emissions from these displays, and the National Environment Agency has not observed significant impacts on air quality arising from them. The Minister did not provide specific estimates for emissions from major events like the NDP or daily shows such as Wings of Time, nor did she confirm plans to replace fireworks with lower-emission alternatives like drone light shows in the future.

results.title: Expanding CDC Voucher Usage from Participating Supermarkets to Heartland Merchants
results.summary: Since January 2023, CDC Voucher allocations have been split equally between participating supermarkets and heartland merchants to balance supporting daily expenses with aiding local businesses. High utilisation rates at both categories confirm the current equal split remains fit for purpose. Consequently, the government will maintain these scheme parameters without further changes. The arrangement accommodates residents shopping at supermarkets while continuing to support heartland merchants and hawkers.

results.title: Expanding Vehicle-specific Data Collection by LTA for More Offence Types
results.summary: The Land Transport Authority's ERP 2.0 system currently supports enforcement for traffic offences like speeding, though further enhancements are required to expand this capability. Data usage remains governed by existing laws, including the Public Service (Governance) Act, and the Second Reading of the Land Transport and Related Matters Bill provides additional details on enforcement scope. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that while the system is primarily designed for payment and charges, it can be utilized for enforcement beyond non-payment of ERP charges.

results.title: Expediting Citizenship Applications, Subsidised Healthcare Rates and Other Moves as Support for Singaporeans Adopting Foreign Children
results.summary: The Minister clarified that adoptive parents may submit citizenship applications after the court grants the adoption order, though processing times vary based on individual criteria. Subsidised healthcare rates and MediShield Life coverage remain strictly reserved for Singaporeans and Permanent Residents, excluding adopted foreign children regardless of status; however, financial assistance is available through medical social workers for those facing challenges. Levy concessions for migrant domestic workers are automatically granted upon the child's citizenship approval, with the Ministry of Manpower considering appeals for retrospective concessions for workers hired to care for adopted children.

results.title: Exposure of Singapore's Reserves to Proposed Changes to Section 892 of US Tax Code
results.summary: The Government expects GIC and Temasek to operate commercially, comply with US laws, and monitor regulatory changes to adjust portfolios as needed. While GIC and Temasek may provide feedback on proposed US tax code changes, including those under Section 892, the Government maintains regular engagement with the US Treasury on financial and tax regulations. The Ministry retains confidence that these investment entities possess the necessary capabilities and risk-management frameworks to assess implications and take appropriate steps to protect Singapore's investment interests. Specific details regarding exposure assessments or representations made before the 13 February 2026 deadline were not provided in the response.

results.title: Extension of LPA Form 1 Filing Fee Waiver beyond 31 March 2026 Deadline and Plans to Encourage Singaporeans to Execute LPAs
results.summary: The Ministry for Social and Family Development confirmed there are no plans to extend the waiver of the Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) Form 1 filing fee beyond 31 March 2026. Consequently, citizens are urged to execute their LPAs immediately to utilize the remaining waiver period before the deadline expires. No alternative measures or incentives to encourage LPA execution beyond the existing fee waiver were announced during the session.

results.title: Factors Determining Requirement of Shadow Teacher or In-class Support Aide for Students and Financial Assistance for Lower-income Families
results.summary: Schools determine the need for shadow teachers or in-class support aides based on individual student needs rather than formal diagnoses, utilizing targeted interventions like the TRANSIT programme in primary schools. For students facing significant adaptation difficulties, parents may engage a time-bound Transition Aide Support in consultation with the school to build independence before potential placement in Special Education schools. The Ministry does not support prolonged aide engagement for such cases. Families unable to afford private aides receive necessary school-based support, including in-class or pull-out assistance from Special Educational Needs Officers, ensuring lower-income students still access appropriate educational interventions.

results.title: Factors for Continued Decline in Full-time Permanent Employment Rate for Polytechnic Graduates Since 2022
results.summary: The decline in full-time permanent employment rates for polytechnic graduates since 2022 reflects a moderation from post-pandemic hiring surges and cautious sentiment amid economic uncertainty, rather than a long-term trend, as outcomes have remained stable over the last decade with approximately nine in ten graduates employed within six months. Entry-level vacancies stood at 39,000 in September 2025, up from 31,000 in June 2025, while the Ministry maintains tight S-Pass dependency ratio ceilings at 10% for services and 15% for other sectors alongside progressive increases to the S-Pass qualifying salary to protect local wages. The Ministry continues to review polytechnic curricula for industry readiness and collaborates with partners to provide internships, while graduates seeking assistance can access career services at their polytechnics, Workforce Singapore, or NTUC's Employment and Employability Institute.

results.title: Feasibility Assessment of Existing Bus Service along Buangkok Green to Connect Buangkok Crescent to Ang Mo Kio MRT Station
results.summary: The Ministry assessed looping Service 88 to Buangkok Crescent, finding it would benefit approximately 150 residents but significantly increase travel time and fares for over 4,000 existing commuters. Additionally, the route extension would exceed the two-hour travel time limit recommended by the Bus Safety Tripartite Taskforce. To address connectivity needs, Service 114 was extended to Yio Chu Kang MRT station, allowing passengers to reach Ang Mo Kio via the North-South Line.

results.title: Finding on Consumption of Infant Formula Products Contaminated with Cereulide Toxins
results.summary: As of 30 January 2026, the Singapore Food Agency (SFA) and Communicable Diseases Agency (CDA) identified three infants with mild symptoms from consuming cereulide-contaminated formula; all have recovered. Following overseas alerts in early January 2026, SFA stepped up surveillance, directed recalls of affected products, and enhanced testing for this uncommon contaminant. The CDA collaborates with healthcare providers to monitor for potential poisoning cases. SFA maintains that effective manufacturing controls should prevent cereulide presence in infant formula.

results.title: Five-Year Statistics on Medisave and MediShield Life Coverage Adequacy towards Lifelong Dialysis Treatment and Healthcare Financing Enhancements for Working Patients
results.summary: Medisave withdrawal limits and MediShield Life coverage for dialysis are regularly reviewed to ensure affordability. MediShield Life claim limits increased by approximately 60% from $1,100 to $1,750 per month starting April 2025. Additionally, Government subsidies for subsidised dialysis patients were enhanced from 1 July 2025. Eligible patients may also access charity assistance and MediFund, with most subsidised patients paying no cash out of pocket. No patient will be denied appropriate care due to inability to pay.

results.title: Guidance to Schools to Encourage Students to Speak up about School-related Issues through Appropriate Channels
results.summary: The Ministry of Education provides schools with guidance to foster a positive culture encouraging student voice through respectful dialogue with teachers, parents, and peers via various platforms. Schools retain autonomy to determine specific approaches, including dialogues, focus groups, and online channels like surveys or forums. The Minister confirmed that students will not be discouraged or penalised for respectfully expressing concerns or views in the public interest.

results.title: Health Impacts of Long-term MRT Noise Exposure for Commuters and Inclusion in National Health Prevention Strategies
results.summary: The National Environment Agency enforces environmental noise limits in residential areas based on international health standards, while the Land Transport Authority monitors MRT cabin noise, maintaining average levels below 85 decibels. Momentary spikes above this threshold occur during tight curves, prompting specific mitigation measures on the Thomson-East Coast Line, including speed moderation, track grinding, and trials of tuned mass dampers. Both agencies will continue reviewing noise management policies to ensure effectiveness in protecting public health. The Minister did not confirm whether MRT noise exposure is currently included in national health promotion strategies or if a dedicated interagency initiative for public education has been established.

results.title: Impact Assessment of Hainan Free Trade Port on Potential Trade Flow Disruption for Singapore
results.summary: The Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Trade and Industry stated that the Hainan Free Trade Port offers zero tariffs, improved customs clearance, and tax incentives for goods with at least 30% value-added processing in Hainan. Singapore's exposure to potential trade disruption is likely limited because existing agreements, including the China-Singapore Free Trade Agreement which eliminates tariffs for 95% of exports, already secure market access. The government will continue monitoring trade patterns and adjusting strategies as necessary.

results.title: Impact of Progressive Wage Model on Wage Levels, and Business Costs and Competitiveness
results.summary: The Minister stated that lower-wage workers outside the Progressive Wage Model benefit from the Local Qualifying Salary and Progressive Wage Mark schemes, driving a 10.1% cumulative rise in real gross monthly income at the 20th percentile from 2021 to 2025, which outpaced the median's 7.4% increase. To address business cost concerns, the Government introduced the Progressive Wage Credit Scheme in 2022 to co-fund wage increases for lower-wage workers, who constitute about one-fifth of the resident workforce. Additionally, employers can access the Workfare Skills Support (Basic) scheme for absentee payroll when sending eligible lower-wage workers for training to support upskilling and sustainable wage growth.

results.title: Impact of US' Withdrawal from International Bodies on Singapore's Interests
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs stated that the US withdrawal from 66 international organisations, including 31 UN entities, has limited immediate direct impact on Singapore but poses long-term risks to global commons management regarding climate change and pandemics. Singapore will continue collaborating with like-minded countries to sustain two US-linked entities headquartered locally: the ReCAAP Information Sharing Centre and the Global Forum on Cyber Expertise (GFCE) Southeast Asia Hub, noting minimal expected impact on their regional funding and operations. The Government remains committed to the UN Charter and will encourage constructive collaboration with the US within remaining major international entities while shaping new partnership platforms. Member states' annual UN budget contributions follow an agreed scale reviewed every three years, with Singapore maintaining strong support for international law and the multilateral system.

results.title: Impact of US-based MNCs' Exemption from 15% Global Corporate Tax on Singapore's Tax Revenues and Medium-term Fiscal Risks
results.summary: The Ministry confirmed that the OECD's exemption for US multinational corporations from the 15% global minimum tax under Pillar Two does not impact Singapore's implementation of its Domestic Top-up Tax. Consequently, all large multinational enterprises, including US-based entities, operating in Singapore must still pay the minimum effective tax rate of 15% on profits earned locally. This arrangement allows US MNEs to continue under the existing US global tax system while maintaining Singapore's fiscal stance. The Minister noted that global competition for investments remains intense, necessitating continued investments to ensure competitiveness and job creation for Singaporeans despite these developments.

results.title: Implementation of Clearer Accountability Measures and Formal Documentation for Extended or Irregular Train Service Wait Times for Affected Commuters
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that commuters affected by train service disruptions can obtain travel chits or delay slips from station staff or rail operators' websites. These documents serve as official proof to employers or schools, protecting commuters from penalties for delays beyond their control. The Ministry has clarified that these slips are available upon request and function as the primary accountability measure for irregular wait times. No new automated late slip notices or specific timelines for issuance were introduced during the session.

results.title: Implementation of Coaching, Safety and Governance Standards across Commercial and Community Sports Providers
results.summary: The Ministry encourages voluntary adoption of coaching, safety, and governance standards via the National Registry of Coaches (NROC), National Registry of Exercise Professionals (NREP), and the Safe Sport Unified Code under SportSG. While adherence is not currently mandated for private entities, public agencies and some National Sports Associations, such as the Singapore Sailing Federation, have adopted these standards by engaging only registered professionals. The Singapore Sailing Federation specifically requires full NROC membership for all its coaches starting 1 July 2025. The Ministry continues to engage the sector to promote these standards and monitor their impact without imposing mandatory requirements at this stage.

results.title: Implementing Progressive Wage Credit Scheme as a More Long-term Feature
results.summary: The Progressive Wage Credit Scheme, introduced in 2022 as a transitionary support measure, aims to help businesses adjust to lower-wage worker policy moves while encouraging investment in employee upskilling. The Minister for Manpower stated the scheme is currently under review to determine its future status, with updates to be provided in due time. This initiative supports sustainable wage increases by transforming businesses and improving productivity, rather than serving as a permanent feature.

results.title: Implications for Singapore from Disputes over US Claim on Greenland
results.summary: The Minister for Foreign Affairs stated that Singapore reaffirms the importance of international law and the UN Charter, emphasizing respect for sovereignty and the right to self-determination. Consequently, any issues concerning Greenland's future must be resolved peacefully in accordance with these principles. Singapore values its longstanding partnership with the United States, which plays a vital role in Asia Pacific security, while maintaining friendly ties with European countries like Denmark. The Government will continue to uphold international law and multilateralism, as these principles protect small states and contribute to global peace and stability.

results.title: Implications for Singapore's Carbon Tax and Sustainability Pathway Given Recent Moves by the US and Partners in Europe and Region
results.summary: The Government adopts a pragmatic, calibrated approach to the carbon tax trajectory, balancing international climate action pace, economic competitiveness, and decarbonisation technology progress. While global climate efforts may fluctuate, the Minister emphasized that severe climate impacts will eventually necessitate drastic action, making decarbonisation essential for Singapore's long-term competitiveness as an energy-disadvantaged nation. Consequently, the carbon tax must remain sufficiently high to incentivise emission-reduction investments and allow for early planning of solutions with longer implementation lead times. The Government also prioritises avoiding carbon leakage by pricing carbon competitively against other jurisdictions, while the Ministry of Trade and Industry actively engages businesses to address concerns and provide necessary support.

results.title: Improvements of Early Morning Train Service Reliability for Essential and Shift Workers
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport stated that train frequencies during off-peak periods, including pre-peak, operate at approximately five to seven minutes and are adjusted based on anticipated loading. The Land Transport Authority monitors rail operators' adherence to schedules, noting that 2025 punctuality standards were met across all lines. Operational variances in intervals may occur to ensure safe passenger boarding and alighting. Commuters can access real-time arrival timings via digital displays at all station entrances and platforms.

results.title: Institutionalising Office of the Leader of the Opposition in Constitution
results.summary: The Government supports, in principle, institutionalising the Office of the Leader of the Opposition (LO) in the Constitution but advises allowing current Parliamentary conventions to mature before codification. The LO is normally the leader of the main opposition party with a significant number of MPs, provided they uphold high standards of honesty, integrity, and command Parliament's trust. If the main opposition leader does not meet criteria, another opposition MP may be considered. The Government considers a scenario where no elected opposition MP meets the requirements as highly unlikely, noting rules ensuring 12 non-ruling party MPs remain in the House to guarantee diverse opposition voices.

results.title: Investigation Panel Report of Tanjong Katong Road South Sinkhole Incident on 26 July 2025
results.summary: The Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment will share the outcome of its internal investigations into the Tanjong Katong Road South sinkhole incident once ready. Concurrently, the Building and Construction Authority is conducting a separate investigation under the Building Control Act. No specific timeline for the completion of either investigation or the release of the independent panel's report has been provided. The Minister did not share any preliminary results or findings regarding the incident at this time.

results.title: LTA's Role and Processes with Respect to Vehicle Recalls
results.summary: Acting Minister for Transport Jeffrey Siow clarified that vehicle manufacturers, motor dealers, and parallel importers bear legal responsibility for identifying affected vehicles, notifying owners, and arranging rectification of safety defects. The Land Transport Authority (LTA) facilitates this process by publishing recall notices on the OneMotoring website upon receipt of reports from manufacturers or dealers. Owners receiving notices are expected to collaborate with their dealers to promptly address any identified safety-related defects. No review of LTA's role or processes regarding vehicle recalls was proposed or confirmed in the response.

results.title: MAS and Life Insurance Association of Singapore (LIAS) Collaboration on Clinical Guidance for Insurance Medical Record Disclosure Context Requirements for Claims
results.summary: Under the Healthcare Services Act (HCSA) and the Health Information Bill, clinicians must maintain patient confidentiality and are strictly prohibited from accessing the National Electronic Health Record (NEHR) for insurance purposes. The Ministry of Health, supported by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) and the Life Insurance Association of Singapore (LIAS), issued guidance clarifying that when insurance request forms lack context or seek all records, clinicians should request clarifications and prepare separate clinical summaries based on their own records rather than disclosing irrelevant data. A circular to all licensees and a specific guidance note to insurers outline appropriate practices for requesting patient medical records, with an expectation that insurer practices align with these requirements to ensure only relevant information is disclosed for claims.

results.title: Measures to Ensure Meal Quality and Portion Compliance in Revised Budget Meal Scheme
results.summary: From 10 January 2026, budget meals become optional for all HDB coffee shops renewing tenancies, with existing providers continuing until their current lease ends. To incentivize participation, HDB triples rental discount support from one to three years, requiring operators to pass the full discount to eligible stallholders. The scheme mandates three specific meal options: an economy rice set, a halal meal, and a breakfast item, all of which must be full meals without daily quantity limits. Funding is not linked to purchase volume to maintain operational efficiency, and HDB will monitor the scheme's response for potential future adjustments.

results.title: Measures to Increase Charging Points in High Demand HDB Car Parks in View of 60,000 EV Charging Points by 2030 Target
results.summary: The Ministry remains on track to deploy 60,000 EV charging points by 2030, with HDB residents now having access to chargers in nearly every HDB carpark. The Land Transport Authority monitors usage data and collaborates with EV Charging Operators to install additional points at high-demand carparks where electrical capacity permits. Allocation decisions account for residents' growing EV demand, prioritizing locations with sufficient infrastructure capacity to support increased usage.

results.title: Measures to Manage "Tunnel Squatting" across Rail Network
results.summary: Approximately 10% of Singapore's underground rail tunnels traverse marine clay and are subject to regular inspections for tunnel squatting. Enhanced monitoring using advanced instruments, laser profilers, and high-definition image scanners is currently applied to segments between Paya Lebar and Mountbatten stations. Should further maintenance be required on other network portions, the public will be informed early. The Minister confirmed that advanced geotechnical monitoring is integrated into maintenance regimes to ensure structural integrity and minimise service disruptions.

results.title: Measures to Strengthen Socioeconomic Mixing in Oversubscribed Schools
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry will not close off possibilities regarding measures to strengthen socioeconomic mixing in oversubscribed schools. Suggestions under review include setting aside places for students from disadvantaged backgrounds and introducing a socioeconomic tie-breaker for balloting instances. The Minister confirmed that all suggestions, including the Member's, will be considered as part of the ongoing refinement to the PSLE and Secondary 1 posting framework. No specific timeline or eligibility criteria were provided for these potential measures.

results.title: Make (Singapore) Equities Great Again
results.summary: The Government's $5 billion Equity Market Development Programme (EQDP) addresses concerns regarding market shrinkage, de-listings, and eroding investor confidence, though officials note it may be necessary but insufficient for permanent structural change. While the review group emphasizes reducing market friction, the response underscores that sustainable growth requires enriching the full capital-raising lifecycle, particularly for the Catalist board, rather than relying on quotas or administrative allocation. Recent data indicates the average daily traded value on SGX grew over 20% year-on-year to nearly $1.8 billion in November 2025, marking the highest level since 2010. Officials maintain that improving competitiveness is a continuous journey driven by commercial merit and effective execution, with robust public enforcement and mandatory disclosure on shareholder returns remaining critical to reversing decades of decline.

results.title: Measures to Tackle Increased Numbers of Long-term Unemployed Resident PMETs above 50
results.summary: The long-term unemployment rate for resident PMETs aged 50 and above declined from 1.2% in 2024 to 1.1% in 2025, remaining below the pre-COVID-19 average of 1.3%. The Government supports mature workers through reskilling initiatives like WSG's Mid-Career Pathways Programme, Career Conversion Programmes, SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme, and SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme, alongside employment facilitation services from WSG and the NTUC EEI. A Tripartite Workgroup on Senior Employment has been convened to refresh senior employment policies, with recommendations scheduled for release in the second half of 2026.

results.title: Moderating Shop Rental Increases in Heritage Precincts Such as Chinatown and Kampong Gelam
results.summary: The Government monitors retail rents in heritage districts, noting median increases of around 2% annually in Kampong Gelam, Little India, and Chinatown, which are comparable to Central Area rates and below nominal GDP growth. While some leases in high-footfall streets like Haji Lane saw higher increases of 25% or more due to normalisation from below-market rates, overall rents remain 20% to 60% lower than Central Area conventional retail spaces. The Inter-Agency Task Force for Heritage Businesses, Traditional Activities and Cultural Life supports designated businesses through the SG Heritage Business Scheme, launched in October 2025, which has recognised 42 businesses including 21 in Kampong Gelam and Chinatown. Current plans do not include extending Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty or Seller's Stamp Duty to commercial properties, and the Government continues to collaborate with agencies and alliances to address stakeholder interests and sustain heritage businesses.

results.title: Monitoring Continuity of Sports Participations of DSA Applicants and Minimising Attrition Rates among Late Developers or Less Advantaged Youths
results.summary: The Government does not monitor the continuity of sports participation for Direct Schools Admission (DSA) applicants, including those not admitted, after their schooling years. Beyond DSA, SportSG and the Singapore Sports School (SSP) collaborate with National Sports Associations to provide multiple entry points into national pathways. Talented athletes can join SSP at any developmental stage if they meet performance criteria, while the Multi Sport Academy allows Primary 6 students with strong movement competencies to enrol without specialising in a specific sport. Under the Sport Excellence framework, carded athletes, including late bloomers, receive support for fluctuating performance to sustain competitive participation.

results.title: More Measures to Encourage Seniors to Go for Regular Dental Check-ups
results.summary: The Minister for Health confirmed that Singapore maintains a comprehensive framework to encourage seniors' regular dental check-ups, focusing on preventive care, service accessibility, and financial support. Recent enhancements to Community Health Assist Scheme (CHAS) subsidies for preventive dental procedures were implemented in October 2025, complemented by the expansion of services across over 1,000 CHAS clinics and 15 polyclinics. Key initiatives include the nationwide Project Silver Screen, which screens over 30,000 seniors annually for oral health issues, and the Oral Health Movement 8020, aiming to help older adults retain at least 20 teeth into their 80s. Additionally, the National Dental Centre Singapore offers Tele-dentistry for remote consultations and recently launched a mobile dental clinic to bring care directly to nursing homes and home care residents.

results.title: Mothers Re-entering Workforce through Career Conversion and SkillsFuture Career Transition Programmes for Last Five Years
results.summary: The Minister for Manpower stated that Workforce Singapore and SkillsFuture Singapore do not track whether female participants in the Career Conversion Programme or the SkillsFuture Career Transition Programme are mothers. Consequently, no data exists to quantify the number of mothers who have re-entered the workforce through these specific schemes over the last five years. The agencies explicitly confirmed the absence of this tracking metric, meaning the requested statistics cannot be provided.

results.title: Mitigating Cost Impact on Customers with Beverage Container Return Scheme's Roll-out in April 2026
results.summary: The Government will implement the Beverage Container Return Scheme (BCRS) on 1 April 2026, introducing a 10-cent deposit on eligible containers to mitigate cost impacts on consumers, particularly lower-income households and smaller producers. To ensure affordability, a $2,500 transition grant is provided to cover registration and fees for small producers, while parallel importers face a separate assessment regarding their ~$40,000 monthly fee burden and risk of losing the discount beverage segment. Operational measures include establishing returnable container vending machines (RVMs) and return points, ensuring cash refund options are available from Day 1, and managing potential queues and logistical challenges such as RVM malfunctions. The Government will review the deposit value and criteria if necessary, and will intervene if consumer price increases exceed 3 to 4 cents, though specific actions for price hikes beyond this threshold remain to be detailed.

results.title: New Round of SkillsFuture Funding for Adult Singaporeans amid Rapidly Evolving Job Market Requirements
