results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that SkillsFuture Singapore offers up to 90% course fee subsidies, with the 2024 SkillsFuture Level-Up Programme specifically targeting Singaporeans aged 40 and above to address skills obsolescence. While the Government will continually review support mechanisms for skills upgrading, there are no immediate plans to introduce a new round of general SkillsFuture funding or subsidies for all adult Singaporeans. This decision maintains the current subsidy structure without expanding eligibility to a broader cohort at this time.

results.title: Noise Monitoring Protocols of Construction Sites and Usage of Noise Readings in Addressing Resident Complaints
results.summary: Contractors at construction sites within 150 metres of noise-sensitive premises must install NEA-specified noise meters that continuously record equivalent continuous sound levels (Leq) at five-minute intervals and capture maximum noise spikes (Lmax). NEA assesses compliance against permissible limits under the Environment Protection and Management (Control of Noise at Construction Sites) Regulations, prioritizing Leq data aligned with World Health Organization guidelines for ongoing noise. Strict enforcement actions are taken against contractors exceeding limits, with more severe penalties applied to repeated or prolonged offences. The agency will continue reviewing regulations, including potential Lmax usage, to ensure effectiveness in protecting public health.

results.title: Number of Bidders for School Bus Service Contracts for SPED Schools
results.summary: Social Service Agencies (SSAs) operating Special Education (SPED) schools conduct independent competitive bidding for school bus services, evaluating operators on fare, fleet capacity, SPED-specific needs, and geographical coverage. The Ministry of Education does not oversee individual SSA tender processes, stipulate bidding mechanisms, or track the number of bids submitted. To broaden market access since 2024, the Ministry extended its Public School Bus Advertisement website portal to SSAs and SPED schools, enabling them to invite bids from a wider pool of operators. SSAs utilizing the portal have received multiple bids, and the Ministry will continue encouraging broader adoption of this resource.

results.title: Number of Licences Granted to Massage Establishments in Past Three Years and Vice Activities Detected
results.summary: In 2023, 2024, and 2025, 907, 877, and 868 massage establishment licences were granted respectively, while 45, 40, and 30 vice activities were detected in licensed premises during the same period. These figures exclude open-concept massage establishments, which remain exempt from licensing provided they comply with specific rules preventing vice activities. The Minister for Home Affairs noted that the Police are reviewing regulations to update the regulatory regime and will consult the industry in due course.

results.title: Number of Students Switching Courses of Study in Polytechnics and ITEs, and Costs Incurred
results.summary: Approximately 200 polytechnic and ITE students switch courses annually, primarily because they feel better suited to the new programme. Institutions assess transfer requests holistically, considering academic readiness and student reasons, while providing career guidance to ensure informed decisions. Switching mid-way typically extends graduation time, though tuition fee subsidy durations are usually sufficient to cover the extended period. Students needing extra financial assistance can approach their institutions for support. The Minister noted that subsidy depletion and non-subsidised fees are managed through these institutional assessments.

results.title: Participation Rate and Profile of Participants at Events Organised by People's Association
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Culture, Community and Youth stated that the People's Association organises hundreds of monthly events across Singapore open to all residents regardless of background, age, race, or religion. While specific participation rates and demographic breakdowns by age or race were not provided, the Minister noted that rates vary by constituency based on resident interests. The PA aims to maintain social cohesion and racial harmony by continuously enhancing programmes to align with evolving population needs. No concrete plans to encourage higher participation were detailed beyond this general commitment to relevance.

results.title: Peak Hour Waiting Times and Capacity Improvement Measures for Service 43 at Bus Stops along Buangkok Green after Withdrawal of Service 43M
results.summary: Following the withdrawal of Service 43M, the Ministry added trips to Service 43 to reduce average peak-hour waiting times from 10 minutes to six minutes in the morning and from 13 minutes to eight minutes in the evening. While commuters can generally board the first arriving bus, variance in arrival times persists due to traffic conditions on busy corridors like Serangoon and Yio Chu Kang. The bus operator will continue to optimise operations to improve service regularity and address prolonged waits.

results.title: Percentage of Reported Vaping Activities during and After-operating Hours and Extending Hotline Operations or Automating Vaping Reporting Systems for Timely Enforcement
results.summary: Since enhanced enforcement began on 1 September 2025, approximately 70% of vaping reports are submitted online via go.gov.sg/reportvape, while 30% come through the hotline. Of all submissions, roughly 20% occur outside the hotline's 9.00 am to 9.00 pm operating hours. The Ministry does not plan to extend hotline hours or automate reporting, as all reports—including those submitted after hours—are responded to promptly. The online channel effectively captures after-hours sightings without requiring operational changes.

results.title: Percentage of Sheltered Playgrounds and Outdoor Fitness Stations within HDB Estates and Public Parks
results.summary: HDB and NParks enhance thermal comfort at playgrounds and outdoor fitness stations by locating facilities in building shadow zones and planting trees for shade. Existing funding schemes for Town Councils and NParks allow rejuvenation projects that may include adding shelters or greenery. The Government has not committed to specific retrofitting funds but utilizes current programmes to improve conditions. Sun-shade analysis guides site selection to balance comfort with the outdoor recreational experience.

results.title: PolCam Installations for Plantation Village and Grove Precincts at Tengah
results.summary: Police cameras for Plantation Village and Grove precincts are expected to be progressively installed from March 2026, following delays caused by rectification works to meet operational standards. Unlike standard HDB precincts where cameras are typically installed within a year of permit granting, these sites require infrastructure upgrades first. Deployment prioritizes high-traffic public areas based on operational needs, crime data, and public feedback rather than solely on resident requests for specific danger zones like lift lobbies. The Singapore Police Force incorporates resident feedback into its overall strategy for deterring and solving crime.

results.title: Police Reports on AI-generated Non-consensual Intimate Images on X's Grok Platform
results.summary: As of January 2026, no police reports have been lodged in Singapore regarding AI-generated non-consensual intimate images on X's Grok platform. The Infocomm Media Development Authority is engaging X to address harmful content, noting that X has implemented global measures to prevent Grok from producing revealing images of women and children. The Ministry will continue monitoring the issue and collaborating with X to enhance online safety for Singapore users. No enforcement actions or bans against Grok were stated at this time.

results.title: Policy on Optimal Class Sizes Given Increasingly Complex Challenges Faced by Teachers
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) tailors class sizes to student needs, maintaining around 30 pupils for Primary 1 and 2 while offering smaller classes of up to 10 for TRANSIT classes supporting students with social and behavioural needs. MOE does not view artificial intelligence as a substitute for human teaching, instead integrating it via the AI in Education framework to enhance scaffolding alongside co-curricular activities and Character and Citizenship Education. Resource allocation depends on continuous dialogue between school leaders and HQ to address specific needs, such as deploying more counsellors or special educational needs officers, rather than fixed thresholds. The Ministry operates within budgetary constraints across ministries and is currently reviewing the academic race, CCE, and CCA to optimise holistic development and teacher workload.

results.title: Possibility of Sentient AI Developing in Singapore's Networks and Systems
results.summary: The Government has not conducted studies on sentient AI, as current systems show no evidence of sentience. While acknowledging that significant future capability advances would be a global development, the Ministry focuses on practical safeguards for critical infrastructure like defence, finance, and transport. These measures include strong cybersecurity controls, access restrictions, monitoring, and human oversight to protect Government systems and essential services from potential threats.

results.title: Property Tax Increment on Non-Owner-Occupied Properties to Address Wealth Inequality
results.summary: The Government increased non-owner-occupied residential property tax rates from 10% to 20% in 2023 and 2024, raising the current bracket to 12%–36% to enhance system progressivity. These higher rates apply primarily to investment properties, distinguishing them from owner-occupied units. The Minister confirmed plans to continue reviewing property taxes to maintain a fair and progressive tax framework. No specific future timeline or additional measures were detailed beyond this ongoing review commitment.

results.title: Proportion of Lesson Time Spent on Financial Literacy-related Curricular in Character and Citizenship Education
results.summary: The Minister for Education clarified that Character and Citizenship Education (CCE) allocates approximately five hours at the primary level to financial literacy topics, including needs versus wants and thrift. Beyond CCE, Mathematics covers money concepts at the primary level and interest/profit/loss at the secondary level, while Food and Consumer Education addresses financial behaviour in lower secondary. The Ministry and MoneySense monitor student proficiency through internal surveys, which indicate that most secondary students possess age-appropriate knowledge of compound interest, inflation, and budgeting. Additional external programmes, such as those by the Institute for Financial Literacy for Junior College students, complement the curriculum with investment management skills.

results.title: Proposal for One Budget Meal Option Per Hawker Stall at Centres Managed by NEA and Town Councils
results.summary: The Minister clarified that the National Environment Agency (NEA) does not mandate budget meals at all hawker centres, but requires Socially-conscious Enterprise Hawker Centre (SEHC) operators to propose initiatives ensuring affordability. Currently, SEHC operators must provide at least one value meal per stall, a requirement explained upfront to stallholders before tenancy agreements are signed. Operators retain flexibility to propose specific value meal items and adjust prices periodically based on stallholder feedback, ensuring they do not operate at a loss. NEA will continue to require such affordability initiatives in future tenders for SEHCs, maintaining a variety of price points to meet resident needs.

results.title: Proposal for Property Tax Deferral Scheme to Give Cash-strapped Persons Option to Postpone Paying Property Taxes
results.summary: The Government does not plan to introduce a property tax deferral scheme allowing payment upon future property transaction or bequest, citing risks of accumulated liabilities. Instead, eligible owner-occupied home owners aged 65 and above can currently apply for an interest-free 24-month instalment plan to manage cash flow. The Minister for Finance stated that while long-term deferral is not considered, the Government will continue reviewing existing measures and considering further support for seniors facing financial difficulties.

results.title: Proposal to Include Information such as Compensation Ranges and Flexible Work Arrangement Options in Government Job Postings for Transparency
results.summary: The Public Service Division (PSD) maintains that agencies retain discretion over the specific information included in job postings, with some already disclosing starting salaries or ranges while others highlight Flexible Work Arrangements (FWAs). While FWAs such as part-time work and telecommuting are available, they are not entitlements and remain subject to operational needs. To address jobseeker fatigue, PSD organized the Singapore Public Service Career Fair in August 2025, featuring 70 agencies with over 2,000 vacancies, and continues to enhance the online Careers@Gov portal. The Division will continue reviewing recruitment processes to attract diverse talent and ensure a positive candidate experience.

results.title: Protocols to Ensure Timely Detection, Confirmation and Parent Notification of Gastroenteritis Outbreak in Schools
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the protocols governing the timeframe for confirming gastroenteritis outbreaks and notifying parents were addressed in an oral reply to Parliamentary Questions 14 to 20 on 3 February 2026. The Minister did not provide specific details on whether these protocols were followed in the recent North View Primary School case or confirm if a review of the protocols is underway. Consequently, the specific operational constraints, eligibility conditions, and any stated actions regarding a review remain untracked in this response.

results.title: Public Transport Customer Satisfaction Survey Findings and Publication of 2024 and 2025 Findings
results.summary: The Public Transport Council did not conduct the Public Transport Customer Satisfaction Survey in 2024 due to a methodology review. Instead, the survey was administered in the third quarter of 2025 using the updated framework. Findings from this 2025 survey will be published later in 2026 following the completion of data analysis. The 2024 findings remain unavailable as the survey was not executed that year.

results.title: Quarterly Statistics on Traffic Accidents from 2023 to 2025 Linked to Substance Use and Traffic Police Assessment Protocols for Such Cases
results.summary: The Traffic Police assesses motorists for impaired driving, requiring blood tests if drug or etomidate use is suspected, which can lead to charges for driving under influence. Between 2023 and 2025, 77 accidents were linked to suspected substance use, comprising 25 drug cases, the remainder etomidate cases, and two involving both. Enhanced penalties already exist for causing accidents while under influence, such as up to 10 years imprisonment and a 12-year driving disqualification for first-time offenders convicted of dangerous driving causing death. The Ministry of Home Affairs will continue monitoring penalty adequacy.

results.title: Range of Total Annual Compensation for Political Office Holders from 2018 to 2024
results.summary: The compensation framework for political office holders, established in 2012, remains unchanged with a norm annual salary of $1.1 million for an entry-level Minister, comprising 65% fixed pay and 35% variable pay. Between 2018 and 2024, the variable components included a National Bonus tied to socioeconomic targets (ranging from zero to six months), an Annual Variable Component matching civil servants (zero to 1.5 months), and a Prime Minister-determined Performance Bonus (zero to six months). While political salaries have been static, median and 20th percentile incomes for Singapore Citizen earners increased by 80% and 87% respectively during this period. An independent committee has been appointed to review the framework and will submit recommendations when ready.

results.title: Rationale for Structuring New HDB Shop Leases as Two Three-year Terms and Proposed Penalties for Early Lease Termination
results.summary: HDB structures new shop tenancies as two consecutive three-year terms to encourage sustainable bidding, with rents for renewals after 10 January 2026 based on initial winning bids. Early termination incurs no penalty, requiring only one month's notice, as HDB accommodates genuine business challenges. To support small and medium enterprises against displacement, the Government offers programmes via Enterprise Singapore and HDB to enhance cost competitiveness and productivity. The Minister noted that this approach aligns with market practice while ensuring prudent financial commitments from tenants.

results.title: Land Transport and Related Matters Bill
results.summary: The Land Transport and Related Matters Bill, a joint initiative by the Ministry of Transport and Ministry of Home Affairs, regularises provisions across existing Acts to strengthen active mobility safety and curb misuse of Personal Mobility Aids (PMAs). Key measures include mandating OBU installation for all Singapore-registered vehicles from 1 January 2026, replacing the obsolete ERP 1.0 system with ERP 2.0 using GNSS technology, and requiring a Certificate of Medical Need (CMN) for PMA users on public paths, with exemptions for seniors aged 70 and above. The Bill also prohibits keeping non-compliant devices, reduces PMA speed limits to 6 km/h, and increases penalties for illegal vehicle alterations to $10,000 for individuals. While the Bill decriminalises ERP non-payment, it introduces discretionary vehicle forfeiture and expands LTA's regulatory function to include unsafe device possession, with financial assistance provided to subsidise compliance costs for affected users.

results.title: Reduction in Budget Meal Options for Lower-income Residents with Scrapping of Budget Meal Initiative at HDB Coffee Shops
results.summary: From 10 January 2026, budget meals at HDB coffee shops become optional upon tenancy renewal, ending the mandatory requirement introduced in 2018. To encourage participation, HDB tripled funding support by extending the 5% rental discount from one year to the full three-year term, which operators must pass fully to eligible stallholders. The scheme is not linked to purchase volume due to administrative constraints, and operators must provide three standardised options: an economy rice meal, a halal meal, and a breakfast item. Existing providers will continue until their current tenancy ends, ensuring no sudden reduction in availability. The Ministry will monitor the transition and assess further adjustments based on operator and resident feedback.

results.title: Reforming Hawker Centre Management Model for Improved Efficiency Standards
results.summary: The Minister clarified that NEA manages HDB-owned hawker centres while Town Councils manage HDB-owned ones, with hawkers' associations handling table cleaning. Cleanliness varies across centres due to factors like size, age, and service providers rather than management agency. No plans exist to consolidate ownership under a single government agency or tender cleaning by region. NEA will continue monitoring cleanliness outcomes to ensure dining spaces remain clean and comfortable, emphasizing that all stakeholders must collaborate to uphold standards.

results.title: Reforms to Sustain Emission Reductions in Manufacturing, Power, Waste and Water Sectors
results.summary: Singapore's national carbon emissions fell 5.3% to 55.5 million tonnes in 2023, primarily due to reduced petrochemical output and lower domestic fuel consumption. The Government is progressively implementing decarbonisation plans across manufacturing, power, waste, and water sectors to meet its 2030 and 2035 Nationally Determined Contributions. Initiatives include the Enhanced Resource Efficiency Grant for Emissions to help companies improve energy efficiency and lower product carbon intensity. As a resource-constrained nation, Singapore relies on global technological advancements and international cooperation to decarbonise cost-effectively. The Ministry is closely monitoring global developments in technology, renewable energy, and carbon markets to ensure the most efficient decarbonisation pathways.

results.title: Refreshing Practical Support Measures for New Mothers Given Reasons for Women to Delay Childbirth
results.summary: The Minister explained that women delay childbirth due to career priorities and concerns about managing parenthood. To address this, the Government provides a comprehensive Marriage and Parenthood package covering housing, healthcare, parental leave, and child caregiving. Effective 1 April, parents receive an additional 10 weeks of Shared Parental Leave to bond with newborns. Polyclinics now offer antenatal education and post-natal recovery support, while Family Nexus sites and digital portals like the Families for Life Parenting Portal provide parenting guidance. The Minister confirmed that support measures are regularly reviewed to address evolving parental needs.

results.title: Requiring Developers to Publish Noise Level Reports and Fund Noise Mitigation Measures for Housing and Commercial Projects Near Public Infrastructures
results.summary: The National Environment Agency mandates developers of new residential and noise-sensitive projects near traffic sources to submit Noise Impact Assessments and implement mitigation measures, such as strategic window placement, to meet technical noise limits. While some traffic noise remains inevitable, the Ministry does not currently require developers to publish environmental disclosure reports or fund noise mitigation for pre-existing infrastructure beyond the standard one-year Defects Liability Period. Buyers are instead encouraged to conduct independent noise assessments before purchasing properties, as the existing regulatory framework addresses noise compliance through impact assessments rather than mandatory developer-funded mitigation for legacy infrastructure.

results.title: Review on Local AI Start-up Manus's Acquisition by Meta and Implications on Singapore's AI Ecosystem
results.summary: The Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) confirmed it is not currently reviewing Meta's acquisition of local AI start-up Manus, as the parties have not notified the agency for a merger assessment. CCS stated there is no reason to suggest the transaction poses potential competition issues, though the agency retains the power to intervene if new information arises. Singapore maintains a voluntary merger notification regime designed to balance regulatory oversight with low compliance costs to avoid stifling innovation. The Minister noted that mergers are a common exit strategy in fast-growing sectors like AI, and CCS will continue monitoring the market to ensure competitiveness and facilitate ongoing innovation within the domestic ecosystem.

results.title: Reviewing $5,000 SEN Fund Cap in Light of Rising Private Specialised Support Costs
results.summary: The Ministry of Education states the $5,000 Special Educational Needs (SEN) Fund cap for students with learning and behavioural disabilities remains sufficient based on current utilisation. Revised income criteria for Government bursaries were announced in October 2025, taking effect from the academic year 2026, with implementation starting January 2026 for ITE and April 2026 for polytechnics. Consequently, no data is yet available regarding the number of students receiving both the SEN Fund and Government bursaries under these new criteria, as the new academic year has not commenced for most institutions.

results.title: Reviewing Shared Responsibility Framework to Address Banks' Responsibility for Unauthorised Digital Transactions that Occur Within Seconds
results.summary: Seventy-nine percent of scam cases in the first half of 2025 involved self-effected or authorised transfers induced by deception rather than unauthorised transactions. The Monetary Authority of Singapore notes that distinguishing these induced transfers from legitimate ones remains complex, requiring a balance between security and convenience. Under the Shared Responsibility Framework, banks must block or hold transactions for at least 24 hours when rapid draining of significant balances is suspected, though this may inconvenience legitimate transfers. Banks perform step-up authentication for flagged transactions, send real-time notifications, and attempt to recall funds promptly, but success is not guaranteed if scammers move funds quickly. Compensation assessments consider whether banks fulfilled obligations and if victims reported fraud promptly. Consumers are urged to exercise vigilance, set lower transaction limits, and use MoneyLock to protect funds.

results.title: Rise in Involuntary Part-time or Temporary Employment among Fresh Polytechnic Graduates from 2023 to 2025
results.summary: The Minister for Education declined to provide immediate details on factors contributing to the rise in involuntary part-time employment among fresh polytechnic graduates from 1.5% in 2023 to 2.3% in 2025, or the increase in those not securing employment from 4.7% to 11.5% over the same period. Instead, the Ministry stated it will address these specific questions, along with other related inquiries, at the next available opportunity. Consequently, no official concerns regarding this trend or specific operational measures have been publicly disclosed in this session.

results.title: SPF Property Seizure Statistics over Past Three Years and Efficiency Rate of Return Process
results.summary: The Singapore Police Force does not track the number or proportion of cases involving seized property over the past three years. Established procedures ensure seized items are returned to rightful owners once no longer needed for investigations or prosecution, or upon case conclusion. Urgent return requests must be directed to the investigation officer-in-charge for assessment. Crucially, a Court order is mandatory before any seized property can be released, regardless of the circumstances.

results.title: Safeguards to Prevent Harm to Vulnerable Users of Gen AI Chatbot App
results.summary: IMDA is engaging X regarding harmful content generated by the Grok app, noting that X has implemented global measures to prevent the production of revealing imagery for women and children. Existing safeguards under the Codes of Practice for Online Safety require designated services like X to maintain community guidelines, content moderation, and user reporting mechanisms, alongside annual transparency reports. The upcoming Online Safety (Relief and Accountability) Act will enable victims to seek redress for specified harms such as online harassment and intimate image abuse. Furthermore, the Online Safety Commission is scheduled to be established by June 2026. IMDA will continue monitoring the issue and collaborating with X to enhance online safety for Singapore users on the platform.

results.title: Scheduling System for MRT Train Frequencies, Conditions Triggering Injection of Additional Trains and Reviewing Commuter Feedback
results.summary: MRT trains operate on fixed schedules rather than dynamic ones to maintain smooth flow, with adjustments made in advance by the Land Transport Authority based on demand trends and feedback. Operators must plan schedules to keep crowding below five passengers per square metre, a threshold currently maintained well below to ensure easy boarding. Real-time train injections are avoided during peak periods to prevent disruption, as schedules are not adjusted during service hours. The Authority regularly reviews these thresholds against actual commuter feedback to balance efficiency with passenger comfort.

results.title: Singapore's Participation in US-Led Board of Peace and Considerations on Existing Membership and Fee Structures
results.summary: The Singapore Government is currently assessing an invitation from the United States to join the US-led Board of Peace. The Minister for Foreign Affairs confirmed that the decision involves evaluating specific factors, though these have not yet been detailed. No expected fee structure was provided for either a three-year or indefinite membership term. The government has not yet determined whether to accept the invitation or finalized the associated financial commitments.

results.title: Singapore's Position on Capture of Venezuela's President Nicolas Maduro
results.summary: On 4 January 2026, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated Singapore's grave concern regarding the US military intervention in Venezuela on 3 January 2026, which it deemed contrary to international law and the UN Charter. Singapore consistently opposes such actions, citing precedents like the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the US invasion of Grenada, and has voted accordingly on relevant UN resolutions. The government maintains that a world order based on "might is right" is unstable and dangerous, particularly for small states. Singapore will continue to adopt a principled foreign policy approach aligned with its long-term national interests to uphold the sovereignty and territorial integrity of all states.

results.title: Singapore's Working Hours Compared with OECD Economies and Plans for Moderating Working Hours
results.summary: In 2025, employed residents in Singapore averaged 41.4 weekly hours, a decline from 44.2 hours a decade ago, while per-hour wages remain in the top 10 among OECD countries. The longest hours among professionals, managers, executives, and technicians are recorded in Health Professionals, Production and Specialised Services Managers, and Administrative and Commercial Managers, whereas Protective Services Workers, Drivers, and Clerical Supervisors work longer hours in non-PMET roles. Rather than regulating hours, the Government promotes flexible work arrangements via the Tripartite Guidelines on Flexible Work Arrangement Requests, allowing workers to formally request flexibility based on reasonable business grounds. Additionally, the Government supports enterprise and workforce transformation initiatives to adopt technology and redesign jobs, aiming to improve efficiency, productivity, and work-life harmony without limiting employment options.

results.title: Special Needs Trust Company's Trust Services Take-up Rates by Household Income and Race, and Plans to Boost Services
results.summary: As of 31 December 2025, 1,410 Special Needs Trust Company (SNTC) trusts were established; income data is not collected as it is not a prerequisite for account opening. Chinese clients hold the largest proportion of accounts, followed by Malays and Indians. To boost take-up, particularly among median income families, the Government announced a dollar-for-dollar matching grant of up to $10,000 for lower- and middle-income caregivers of persons with disabilities to set up and top up SNTC trust accounts. This grant, launched later in 2025, aims to encourage more families to utilize these services.

results.title: Stages when Students in Mainstream Schools are Assessed for Special Educational Needs and Support to Help These Students Prepare for School
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) supports students with special educational needs (SEN) in mainstream schools through a staged assessment process that begins before admission via transition information from hospitals and Early Intervention Programme for Infants and Children centres. Upon entering Primary 1, teachers conduct systematic screenings for foundational skills, while SEN officers observe social-behavioural adjustments; students requiring additional support receive targeted interventions like learning support programmes or the TRANSIT scheme. For persistent difficulties, schools refer students with parental consent to MOE Educational Psychologists for diagnostic assessments, which typically conclude within two months, allowing continued support during the evaluation. Transition information is subsequently shared with secondary schools to facilitate resource allocation and class planning, ensuring continuous intervention across educational stages.

results.title: Statistics of Eligibility of Enabling Employment Credit Employers and Barriers Preventing Greater Uptake
results.summary: Employers hiring resident persons with disabilities earning under $4,000 monthly are eligible for the Enabling Employment Credit and are automatically enrolled via CPF contributions. The Minister referenced a written reply from 14 October 2025 detailing barriers to hiring and existing support measures, while noting the Taskforce on Assurance for Families with Persons with Disabilities, announced in December 2025, will study further recommendations to strengthen employer support. These initiatives aim to raise the employment rate for resident persons with disabilities aged 15 to 64 from 34.7% (2024–2025) to a 2030 target of 40%. Specific proportions of employers utilizing the credit or detailed barriers were not provided in this session.

results.title: Statistics of Male Domestic Violence Victims with Protection Orders over Past Five Years and Addressing Under-reporting of Male Victims
results.summary: The Minister for Social and Family Development directed Members to the Domestic Violence Trends Report 2025 for sex-disaggregated data. Between 2020 and 2024, an average of approximately 2,000 Personal Protection Order applications were filed annually, with male victim-survivors accounting for roughly 25% of these cases. The Ministry has not committed to publishing separate gender-disaggregated statistics beyond the referenced report.

results.title: Statistics of Shophouses in Kampong Glam on Wakaf Land and MUIS' Preservation Plans
results.summary: MUIS and its subsidiary Warees Investments manage 26 shophouses in Kampong Glam situated on wakaf land. In preserving the area's cultural identity and historical value, they collaborate with relevant agencies to support local heritage businesses in food, beverage, and retail sectors. Additionally, MUIS and Warees are supporting rejuvenation and placemaking efforts, including the Kampong Gelam Place Plan, to enhance heritage offerings and experiences within the locality.

results.title: Statistics on Discrimination Complaints Received from 2021 to 2025 with Breakdown of Types of Discrimination
results.summary: Between 2021 and 2025, the Tripartite Alliance for Fair and Progressive Employment Practices (TAFEP) received an average of 296 workplace discrimination complaints annually. The Minister for Manpower provided a breakdown of these complaints by type in Table 1, though the specific categories and their corresponding figures are not detailed in the transcript. This data reflects the volume of discrimination cases handled by TAFEP over the five-year period.

results.title: Statistics on Employment Outcomes for SPED Graduates from 2022 to 2024 and Publication of Annual SPED Graduate Employment Data
results.summary: Approximately half of the roughly 500 annual Special Education (SPED) graduates from 2022 to 2024 secured employment, internships, or higher education within six months of graduation, with jobs concentrated in retail, horticulture, and hospitality. While the Ministry of Education collaborates with SG Enable and the Ministry of Social and Family Development to track outcomes via the Disability Trends Report 2024, it has not yet committed to publishing annual SPED graduate employment data comparable to the Graduate Employment Survey. An inter-agency Taskforce on Assurance for Families with persons with disabilities has been launched to study better support mechanisms for sustained employment, with findings and recommendations to be shared in due course.

results.title: Statistics on Pre-tertiary Peer-to-peer Sexual Misconduct Cases Reported and Counselling Support Protocols Available
results.summary: Over the past five years, reported pre-tertiary peer-to-peer sexual misconduct cases at primary and secondary levels have remained low. Schools manage incidents holistically through disciplinary measures, authority reporting, victim support, and student education. Trained school counsellors address social-emotional issues, but if parents decline intervention, schools refer students to social service agencies for specialised support. The Ministry ensures parents are engaged to guarantee their child receives appropriate guidance and intervention.

results.title: Statistics on Private Health Insurance Coverage Disputes Impacting Access to Timely Clinical Treatment over Past Three Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Health (MOH) does not actively track disputes between patients and private health insurers, classifying them as private contract matters. While the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) expects insurers to process claims fairly and will take action against non-compliant entities, specific data on dispute trends over the past three years is not held by MOH. For unresolved claims, policyholders may seek recourse at the Financial Industry Disputes Resolution Centre, with separate data on disputes to be provided by MAS.

results.title: Status of Local Wild Boar Population Following 2023 African Swine Fever
results.summary: Following the 2023 African Swine Fever outbreak, wild boar populations in Pulau Ubin and western Singapore decreased but have since shown signs of recovery. The National Parks Board will continue to monitor these populations and assess impacts on forest biodiversity using a science-based approach. No specific measures to manage potential ecological imbalances were detailed beyond ongoing monitoring. The Minister noted that recovery is underway without specifying a timeline for population stabilization or further intervention plans.

results.title: Summonses Issued to Cyclists for Failing to Display Front and Rear Lights for Night Rides
results.summary: Between 2023 and 2025, 167 summonses were issued to cyclists for failing to display required front white and rear red lights at night, with the majority of offences occurring on roads. The Acting Minister for Transport reported that 42 summonses were issued in 2023, 79 in 2024, and 46 in 2025. To address this, the Government partners with stakeholders to conduct school talks aimed at raising public awareness of safe cycling practices, including the mandatory use of bicycle lights. The transcript does not specify which cyclist groups commit these offences most frequently or provide data on the effectiveness of these education efforts against observed compliance rates.

results.title: Support for Student-Athletes Incurring Additional Tuition or Administrative Fees due to Longer Time-to-graduation
results.summary: Student-athletes in schools applying for Leave of Absence (LOA) to focus on national training or competitions will not incur additional tuition or administrative fees, though a nominal LOA fee may apply to cover administrative costs. Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) offer flexible arrangements including LOA for athletes deferring candidature due to training, ensuring no extra fees are charged. Schools and IHLs may also provide further financial support based on individual circumstances. The Minister confirmed these measures support athletes in balancing sporting and academic pursuits without financial penalty for extended graduation timelines.

results.title: Suspension of Standing Orders to Allow Senior Minister of State and Minister of State to Speak More than Once, and to Remove Time Limit for Speeches
results.summary: The House granted exemption from Standing Order No 48(3) and No 48(8) to allow Senior Minister of State Ms Sim Ann and Minister of State Mr Baey Yam Keng to speak more than once and remove time limits for their speeches during the Second Reading of the Land Transport and Related Matters Bill. Acting Minister Jeffrey Siow will move the Second Reading, addressing amendments on Electronic Road Pricing 2.0 and serious vehicular offences. Ms Sim Ann will cover new offences and penalty increases regarding speed limiters on behalf of the Minister for Home Affairs, while Mr Baey Yam Keng will address active mobility amendments. The exemptions were requested to facilitate responses to Member queries, acknowledging that speeches may exceed standard time limits due to the need for clarifications.

results.title: Take-up Rate of SkillsFuture-supported AI-related Training Course
results.summary: Last year, over 105,000 individuals took up approximately 137,000 training places across 1,600 AI-related courses supported by SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG). While the Ministry does not set specific targets for professionals, managers, and executives, it encourages lifelong learning through substantial fee subsidies and SkillsFuture Credit. The Government is reviewing its framework to better signpost and curate courses, including mapping industry tasks to available training to assist workers in becoming AI-ready. Regarding post-training deployment, the Ministry works with economic agencies to understand how skills are utilized, though tracking specifically for AI courses is challenging due to the varying levels of training, from introductory to transformative career pivots.

results.title: Take-up Rates for Public Transport Vouchers
results.summary: In an exercise concluding October 2025, approximately 360,000 Public Transport Vouchers (PTVs) were issued to 72% of the 500,000 eligible households. To improve take-up, the government eliminated the need for annual re-submissions for past beneficiaries and introduced year-round online applications for new applicants, with immediate SMS notifications upon approval. Each PTV covers roughly half a year of fare increases for a typical four-person household, while additional support is available through Community Centres. Beyond vouchers, the government provides over $2 billion in annual subsidies to fund bus and train services, allowing the Public Transport Council to defer bulk fare increases. Consequently, public transport fares remain among the lowest globally, with the proportion of household income spent on transport falling from 3.1% in 2015 to 2.4% in 2024 for lower-income households.

results.title: Trade Diversification Strategy in Building Supply Chain Resilience through Strengthening International Partnerships
results.summary: Singapore is deepening cooperation with major economies while expanding trade links with emerging markets in Latin America, South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa. The Ministry is strengthening regional and bloc-to-bloc engagements, including upgrading the ASEAN Trade in Goods Agreement and ongoing dialogues for CPTPP-ASEAN and CPTPP-EU collaboration. In September 2025, Singapore, Switzerland, New Zealand, and the UAE launched the Future of Investments and Trade Partnership, an informal group of 16 trade-dependent economies to collaborate on supply chain resilience and technology. Additionally, in October 2025, Singapore and New Zealand agreed on a legally binding supply chain resilience agreement under their Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, allowing essential goods trade during crises, a first-of-its-kind for Singapore. The government will continue exploring similar agreements with other like-minded economies to protect access to critical supplies.

results.title: Transition Planning of Students with Special Educational Needs from Primary to Secondary School under Optional Formal Declaration Framework
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the Ministry of Education's reply to oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 30 and 31 on 3 February 2026 addresses how secondary schools utilize information on students with special educational needs. This information supports early intervention planning, resource allocation, class placement, and continuity of support preparations for students entering Secondary 1 under the optional formal declaration framework. The specific operational details regarding these processes were not elaborated in this transcript but are contained within the referenced parliamentary response.

results.title: Trend of Foreign Technology Companies Re-domiciling in Singapore to Mitigate Geopolitical Regulatory Risks
results.summary: Foreign technology companies re-domiciling in Singapore must register with the Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority and comply with local laws. Re-domiciled entities must meet minimum requirements for substantive business activity, including total assets, revenue, or employment, alongside solvency and legality standards. The Government does not require companies to disclose reasons for re-domiciling applications. This regulatory framework reinforces Singapore's status as an open economy and trusted business hub while contributing to local employment creation and strengthening the technology ecosystem.

results.title: Update on "My Healthy Plate" Guidelines for Ultra-Processed Food Consumption Tracking and Health Impact among Singaporeans
results.summary: The Ministry assesses that ultra-processed foods currently contribute less than one-third of Singapore residents' total caloric intake, a figure lower than in Western nations like the UK and US. While the 2025 US Dietary Guidelines prioritize whole foods, the Ministry maintains that existing My Healthy Plate guidelines already discourage ultra-processed foods due to their unfavourable nutrient profiles, such as high caloric density and low fibre. The Health Promotion Board will continue monitoring international evidence and encourage industry reformulation to improve food products through the Healthier Choice Symbol programme. No specific timeline for updating the My Healthy Plate guidelines to explicitly target ultra-processed foods was stated.

results.title: Update on LUP Status for Blocks Determined to be Unfeasible and Application Rate for Enhanced LHG
results.summary: In 2025, the Lift Upgrading Programme (LUP) was announced for six blocks, with plans to progressively extend it to approximately 40 more blocks. About 100 blocks remain without LUP due to high costs exceeding $200,000 per unit, prompting HDB to explore new technologies for feasible direct lift access. To assist residents with mobility challenges in these unfeasible blocks, the Lift Access Housing Grant (LHG) was enhanced from $30,000 to $80,000 in April 2025 for purchasing flats with direct lift access. In the eight months following this enhancement, HDB received 18 LHG applications, an increase from the average of 11 annual applications prior to the change.

results.title: Updates on Beverage Container Return Scheme Point Locations and Infrastructure Planning
results.summary: The Minister for Sustainability and the Environment stated that the question regarding the identification of approximately 1,000 Beverage Container Return Scheme return points and the prioritisation of high-footfall HDB blocks has been addressed in the Ministry's combined answer to Questions 1 to 5 on the Order Paper for 3 February 2026. Details on exploring tie-ups with existing commercial parcel locker or collection-point machines to leverage existing infrastructure were not provided in this response. Users should refer to the full parliamentary record for the complete answer.

results.title: Usage Statistics of 2026 CDC Vouchers Spent on Heartland Businesses and Impact on Household Consumption and Small Business Resilience
results.summary: The 2026 CDC Vouchers Scheme (January) tranche was launched a month ago, with specific claim and spend rates for this latest batch not yet available. Over the preceding four years, $3.995 billion in CDC and SG60 vouchers were spent, with $2.127 billion directed at heartland merchants and hawkers versus $1.868 billion at supermarkets. The Government assesses the scheme's impact as boosting daily necessities for households while enhancing the resilience and vibrancy of participating heartland businesses.

results.title: Value of Locally Produced High-end AI Chips and Efforts to Overcome Existing Key Constraints from 2020 to 2025
results.summary: Singapore's semiconductor output expanded from $100 billion in 2020 to $133 billion in 2024, though product-specific data remains undisclosed. High-end chipmaking faces intense global competition and constraints related to land and energy intensity, requiring selective project pursuit. The government employs a multi-pronged strategy to overcome these challenges by expanding manufacturing footprints for leading firms, evidenced by KLA's new advanced inspection facility and Micron's $30 billion NAND flash plant. Efforts also include promoting research and development activities for companies and start-ups, exemplified by A*STAR's partnership with GlobalFoundries for advanced packaging and silicon photonics. Additionally, national platforms like A*STAR's National Semiconductor Translation and Innovation Centre are being established to support translational research and deepen technological capabilities within the high-end chipmaking ecosystem.

results.title: Verifying that Subsidised Courses for Licensed Professions are Aligned with Prerequisite Qualifications
results.summary: SkillsFuture Singapore (SSG) requires training providers to submit evidence of support from relevant Government agencies when applying for funding for courses supporting licensed occupations. Learners can verify alignment with prerequisite qualifications by checking specific licensing requirements on the MySkillsFuture portal or identifying courses via the Progressive Wage Model (PWM) tag. This process ensures subsidised courses for licensed professions are validated against regulatory standards before enrolment.
